statement.markdown: Given a string representing a sequence of HTML tags, validate that the tags are properly nested and the closing tags match the opening tags. The string will only contain HTML tags, which are represented as '<tag_name>' or '<tag_name>content</tag_name>'. The function should return True if the HTML is valid, False otherwise. Note that the order of the tags does not matter.
statement.outputFormat: True if the HTML is valid, False otherwise
statement.notes: The function should handle cases where there are multiple opening tags without matching closing tags.

constraints.description: The number of HTML tags in the input string

examples.explanation: The HTML is well-formed and properly nested.
The closing body tag is missing.
The HTML is well-formed and properly nested, even with a script tag inside the body tag.

hints.text: Start by creating a stack to keep track of the opening tags.
Iterate through the string and push opening tags onto the stack.
When encountering a closing tag, check if the stack is empty or the top of the stack does not match the closing tag.

editorial.title: Validating Nested HTML Tags
editorial.intuition: Use a stack to keep track of the opening tags and check for matching closing tags.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the string and manually check for matching tags, but this approach will be inefficient for large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a stack to keep track of the opening tags and check for matching closing tags.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a stack to keep track of the opening tags and check for matching closing tags, with a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of HTML tags.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The stack will always be empty at the end if the HTML is valid, and will contain unmatched opening tags if the HTML is invalid.
editorial.dryRun: Start with an empty stack and iterate through the string, pushing opening tags onto the stack and checking for matching closing tags.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for unmatched opening tags
Failing to check for mismatched closing tags
